root_cause_hypothesis: orders, items and payments are stored at different grains
business_impact: Naive join inflates product revenue +4.5%, cash +26.9%
dashboard_impact: revenue and payment KPIs inflate (~+4.5% product, +27% cash) if joined directly
treatment: Aggregate items & payments to order grain before joining
open_question: Should freight sit inside published GMV or only as a separate dashboard line?

root_cause_hypothesis: geolocation logs many lat/lng pings per zip prefix, not one canonical point
dashboard_impact: any geo map/aggregate explodes ~53× if joined un-deduped; state revenue is read from customers.customer_state instead
treatment: Dedupe to one row per prefix (mean lat/lng, modal city/state)
open_question: Is modal city/state per prefix accurate enough for a city-level map, or is a gazetteer needed?

root_cause_hypothesis: Olist issues a fresh customer_id per order; customer_unique_id is the stable person key
dashboard_impact: using customer_id as the person zeroes the 3.12% repeat rate and overstates customer count by ~3,345
open_question: With no contact keys, could customer_unique_id still under-count repeats across long gaps?

root_cause_hypothesis: the review comment is an optional free-text field; most buyers leave a score only
business_impact: Voice-of-customer limited to a validated sample
dashboard_impact: voice-of-customer panels reflect a self-selected 41% subset, not all customers
treatment: Scope text analysis to the 40,977 with text; flag, don't impute
open_question: Are silent (score-only) customers systematically happier, biasing the text sample negative?

issue_description: Missing delivery dates: delivered 2,965; carrier 1,783; approved 160
root_cause_hypothesis: delivery timestamps populate only once an order reaches that stage; missing = not-yet/never delivered
business_impact: Delivery metrics computed on delivered-with-dates only
dashboard_impact: treating missing as 0-day delivery would understate median delivery (10.2 d) and overstate the on-time rate
treatment: Tie to non-delivered status; exclude from delivery KPIs
open_question: Should canceled/undelivered orders surface as a separate 'fulfilment gap' KPI rather than be dropped?

root_cause_hypothesis: 610 products were never tagged with a category in the source catalog
dashboard_impact: an 'unknown' slice (~1.85% of products) appears in the category Pareto; dropping it would hide real revenue
treatment: Map to English where possible; bucket nulls as 'unknown'
open_question: Can the missing categories be inferred from description length/dimensions to shrink the 'unknown' bucket?

issue_description: 2 PT categories absent from translation (pc_gamer, portateis_...)
root_cause_hypothesis: two Portuguese categories (pc_gamer, portateis_...) were absent from the official translation table
dashboard_impact: without the manual labels these categories drop out of the English Pareto; 74 vs 72 categories reconciled
open_question: Are the two hand-added labels the names stakeholders expect, or should they match an internal taxonomy?

root_cause_hypothesis: a few legacy/adjustment rows carry non-positive values or an undefined payment type
dashboard_impact: negligible effect on totals; keeps payment-mix and cash KPIs clean
treatment: Flag and exclude from cash metrics
open_question: Do the ≤0 rows represent refunds/chargebacks that belong in a separate returns metric?

issue_description: 775 orders with no item row; 1 with no payment
root_cause_hypothesis: 775 orders never had an item row (canceled/unavailable before completion); 1 lacks a payment
business_impact: Revenue computed on orders with items
dashboard_impact: including them would add zero-revenue orders and dilute AOV
open_question: Should these surface as a 'failed order' operational metric rather than be silently excluded?

root_cause_hypothesis: topic and sentiment are not in the data; they are model-inferred from review text
business_impact: Topic kappa 0.81, sentiment kappa 0.89, star agree 83%
dashboard_impact: the topic breakdown (product 52% / delivery 38% of negatives) is directional from a 2.4% sample, not a full-population count
treatment: Validated vs independent 2nd LLM annotator + star rating
open_question: Does full-population API classification of all 40,977 reviews shift the product-vs-delivery split?

field_or_table: order_items/order_payments/order_reviews vs orders; order_items vs products
issue_description: items/payments/reviews vs orders; items vs products
root_cause_hypothesis: marketplace tables could in principle reference missing parents (broken foreign keys)
business_impact: Zero orphans across all four checks
dashboard_impact: zero orphans found, so joins are safe and no rows are silently lost from KPIs
open_question: Re-run the anti-joins whenever new monthly data lands, to catch drift?
